strengths: Seven concrete pages with distinct layout patterns
Hits every explicit seed requirement: menu, library with filters, calendar with brackets, booking flow, loyalty signup, new-release reviews
Motif is specific: hex-tile grids, dice-face dividers, sticker price tags, meeple bullets
Realistic content model with shelf location, checked-out flag, complexity 1-5, mood tags, deposit
Interactions feel earned: availability badge per card, stepper booking with sticky summary, points calculator
Mobile behavior is thought-through, not boilerplate
weaknesses: Pawn/Knight/Queen tier names borrow from chess, mild theme drift
Blog rubric categories overlap with library mood tags
Three rooms named but differentiation not surfaced in booking step content

strengths: Publisher-first angle gives catalog real purpose (retailer locator, rulebook PDFs, designer diaries)
Stamp-card loyalty motif is fresh and tactile
Illustrated floor-plan booth picker is a memorable, domain-specific interaction
League standings plus bracket viewer with season switcher is concrete
Menu pairing toggle cleverly cross-links catalog and tavern
weaknesses: Only four flagship games undercuts the seed emphasis on library search with filters
Tavern and menu merged into one page deviates from seed's explicit menu/drinks page
Some sections lean on generic patterns (FAQ accordion, archive list, hours strip)
Designer roster on blog and league signup feel underdeveloped
